comment: 12yo female with classic Peutz-Jeghers mucocutaneous phenotype — prominent perioral and buccal mucosal melanin macules (lentiginous pigmentation) since early childhood + digital-tip pigmentation. Index small-bowel intussusception age 9 requiring emergency surgical reduction (histopathology: jejunal hamartomatous polyp consistent with PJS). Sibling and paternal aunt with similar perioral pigmentation, untested. No current cancer diagnosis. Engine fires RF-PEUTZ-JEGHERS-FAMILY-HISTORY-SUSPICION via classic phenotype + intussusception-history branches and routes to PreventionPlan: standard = IND-PEUTZ-JEGHERS-SUSPICION-PREVENTION-GENETIC-COUNSELING (pre-test counseling + germline STK11 panel); alternative = IND-PEUTZ-JEGHERS-SUSPICION-PREVENTION-ENHANCED-SURVEILLANCE (empirical multi-organ PJS surveillance). Distinct from PEUTZ-JEGHERS-PREV-001 (14yo + parent affected) — sibling/aunt second-degree pattern instead of first-degree.
